_comment: Single source of truth for Yamete's mutation-test catalog. Each entry encodes one production gate that has a corresponding behavioural test cell. The runner (scripts/mutation-test.sh) applies each mutation to a clean working tree, runs the named XCTest, asserts the test FAILS with the expected substring, then reverts via git. See Tests/Mutation/README.md for full architecture.

mutations.description: TrackpadActivitySource.handleTapDown — drop tap-attribution recency gate; external mouse clicks must not be credited as trackpad taps.
MouseActivitySource scrollMonitor — drop empty-phase guard; trackpad-flavoured (phased) scrolls must not be credited as mouse scrolls.
TrackpadActivitySource.handleScrollEvent — drop the phase guard around lastTrackpadGestureAt stamp; mouse-wheel events must not register the user's hand as on the trackpad.
KeyboardActivitySource — change tapRateThreshold default from 3.0 to 0.0; below-threshold rates must not fire keyboardTyped.
mutations.replace: private var tapRateThreshold: Double = 0.0  // key presses / second
mutations.search: private var tapRateThreshold: Double = 3.0  // key presses / second

mutations.description: TrackpadActivitySource.handleTapDown — drop the per-mode enable gate; toggling tappingEnabled=false must suppress all .trackpadTapping reactions.
TrackpadActivitySource.handleScroll — drop empty-phase guard; mouse-wheel scrolls (phase==[]) must not drive trackpad scroll RMS / contact / circle paths.
MouseActivitySource.handleMouseScroll — invert magnitude floor so all events drop; sustained high-magnitude wheel events must fire .mouseScrolled, so dropping all events fails the cell.
mutations.replace: if true { return }  // mutated: drop ALL events
mutations.search: guard mag > 0.5 else { return }  // filter tiny accidental movements

mutations.description: MouseActivitySource.handleHIDClick — drop transport/product attribution filter; SPI built-in trackpads and USB Magic Trackpad clicks must NOT fire .mouseClicked.
MouseActivitySource.handleHIDClick — slam click-gate shut so no click ever fires; legitimate USB mouse clicks must publish .mouseClicked.
TrackpadActivitySource.handleTapDown — slam tap-rate / debounce gate shut; gesture+click within attribution window must still fire .trackpadTapping.
mutations.search: guard rate >= tapMin, now >= tappingGate else { return }

mutations.description: TrackpadActivitySource.evaluateScroll — drop the per-mode sliding enable gate; toggling slidingEnabled=false must suppress all .trackpadSliding reactions.
TrackpadActivitySource.evaluateScroll — drop the per-mode touching enable gate; toggling touchingEnabled=false must suppress all .trackpadTouching reactions.
TrackpadActivitySource.handleScroll — slam tiny-magnitude filter shut; sustained scroll above touching threshold must still fire .trackpadTouching.
KeyboardActivitySource.handleKeyPress — slam the rate/debounce gate shut; an at-threshold keypress burst must still fire .keyboardTyped exactly once.
mutations.search: guard rate >= tapRateThreshold, now >= typingGate else { return }

mutations.description: KeyboardActivitySource._testEmit — drop the kind-guard so non-.keyboardTyped kinds incorrectly publish .keyboardTyped (the unconditional publish below the guard is hardcoded).
MouseActivitySource.handleMouseScroll — slam the RMS/debounce gate shut; sustained high-magnitude wheel events must still fire .mouseScrolled.
mutations.search: guard rms > scrollThreshold, now >= scrollGate else { return }

mutations.description: TrackpadActivitySource.start — drop the monitor-idempotency guard; a second start() must not double-install NSEvent monitors. Without the guard, every click is processed twice, doubling the tap-rate count and firing .trackpadTapping at half the configured tapMin.

PowerSource.handlePowerChange(onAC:) — drop the edge-trigger guard against lastWasOnAC; same-state IOPS callbacks (battery percent, charge state, time-remaining estimates) must NOT republish .acConnected / .acDisconnected.

PowerSource.start — drop the runLoopSource-nil idempotency guard; a second start() call must NOT re-seed lastWasOnAC from currentlyOnAC(), or a previously-no-op inject becomes a fresh edge and the bus republishes the AC state.
DisplayHotplugSource.dispatchDebounced — drop the 200ms shouldFire window guard; the 3-4 callbacks macOS emits per real reconfigure must collapse to 1 .displayConfigured publish, not 4.
ImpactDetector.process — drop the warmup gate; samples processed before sampleCount reaches warmupSamples must be rejected so filter state can settle.
mutations.search: guard s.sampleCount >= config.warmupSamples else { return nil }

mutations.description: ImpactDetector.process — drop the spike threshold gate; magnitudes below the configured spikeThreshold must NOT detect.
mutations.search: guard magnitude >= config.spikeThreshold else { return nil }

mutations.description: ImpactDetector.process — drop the rise rate gate; a slow ramp whose peak consecutive rise is below minRiseRate must NOT detect (separates impacts from gradual changes).
ImpactDetector.process — drop the crest factor gate; a sample marginally above an elevated background RMS must NOT detect (separates impacts from sustained noise).
ImpactDetector.process — drop the confirmations gate; a single above-threshold sample must NOT meet a multi-confirmation requirement (one-shot transients are noise).
ImpactFusion.start — drop the empty-availability gate; calling start() with zero available sources must invoke onError with noAdaptersAvailable and NOT mark the engine running.
ImpactFusion.ingest — drop the rearm-duration gate; an impact arriving inside the configured rearmDuration after a prior trigger must NOT re-trigger.
mutations.search: guard now.timeIntervalSince(lastTriggerAt) >= config.rearmDuration else { return nil }

mutations.description: ImpactFusion.ingest — drop the participating-sources consensus gate; one source reporting against a 2-of-2 consensus requirement must NOT trigger.
mutations.search: guard participatingSources.count >= required else { return nil }

mutations.description: HeadphoneMotionSource.impacts — drop the framework-availability gate; opening the stream when CMHeadphoneMotionManager reports motion unavailable must throw SensorError.deviceNotFound, not silently start updates.
HeadphoneMotionSource.impacts — drop the mid-stream connection check; samples arriving after the headphones disconnect must be pruned (not delivered to the impact detector).
MicrophoneSource.impacts — drop the input-format validity gate; a driver reporting a zero-channel or zero-sample-rate format must surface SensorError.deviceNotFound and MUST NOT install a tap.

ImpactFusion.stop — drop the isRunning idempotency gate; calling stop() against a not-running engine must take the no-op path (lastStopWasNoOp == true, stopTeardownCount stays 0). Removing the guard would let the teardown body run, bumping stopTeardownCount.

HeadphoneMotionSource.startConnectionProbe — drop the framework-availability gate; constructing the adapter with a driver that reports motion unavailable must NOT call driver.startUpdates from the probe. Removing the guard would let the probe touch the driver against an unsupported framework.

HeadphoneMotionSource.finishProbeIfRunning — drop the probeStage state-machine guard. After impacts() takes the manager (stage == .takenOver), the deferred probe-stop closure must NOT call stopUpdates again or flip the stage to .complete. Removing the guard would invalidate the active impact stream.
mutations.replace: guard stage == .running || true else { return false }

mutations.description: MicrophoneSource.impacts tap callback — slam the frameLength > 0 gate shut so EVERY buffer is dropped. Strong-transient buffers driven through the permissive detector must yield ≥ 1 impact under the production gate; with the gate inverted the loop short-circuits and no impacts are emitted.

AccelerometerReader.ReportContext.handleReport — drop the minimum-length floor; a payload shorter than the BMI286 24-byte report must NOT yield an impact (the misaligned int32 reads at offsets 6/10/14 would otherwise consume garbage).

AccelerometerReader.ReportContext.handleReport — drop the running guard; reports arriving after ctx.invalidate() (the teardown phase-1 silence step) must NOT reach the impact detector or yield onto the continuation.

AccelerometerReader.ReportContext.handleReport — drop the decimation gate; with decimationFactor=2 the cell drives 10 reports and asserts exactly 5 yields. Removing the gate yields on every report (10 yields), failing the equality check.
mutations.search: guard s.sampleCounter % decimationFactor == 0 else { return nil }

mutations.description: AccelerometerReader.ReportContext.handleReport — drop the magnitude bounds gate; sub-floor (rawMag < magnitudeMin) and super-ceiling (rawMag > magnitudeMax) raw vectors must NOT pass through to the bandpass filter and detector.
mutations.search: guard rawMag > magnitudeMin && rawMag < magnitudeMax else { return nil }

mutations.description: AccelerometerReader.ReportContext.surfaceStall — drop the running guard; after ctx.invalidate() a late-arriving stall must NOT re-finish the consumer continuation with a spurious error.
mutations.replace: guard true else { return nil /* already-stalled */ }
mutations.search: guard s.running else { return nil /* already-stalled */ }

mutations.description: AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.evaluateWatchdogTick — drop the running guard; an invalidated snapshot must decode as .invalidated, NOT proceed into staleness math (which could surface a spurious .stalled and re-fire surfaceStall after the consumer has already torn down).

AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.evaluateActivity — drop the dispatchAccel filter; SPU services with dispatchAccel=false (gyro / temperature / hinge-angle siblings) must decode as .skip so the iterator keeps looking for the accelerometer service rather than mis-treating a sibling as the accelerometer.

AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.evaluateActivity — drop the monotonic-clock guard; a non-monotonic snapshot (now ≤ lastTs, e.g. clock reset / overflow) must decode as .clockNonMonotonic, NOT proceed into the (now - lastTs) UInt64 subtraction (which would underflow to a huge bogus deltaNs and decode as .stale).

AccelerometerReader.SensorActivation.activate — drop the IOServiceGetMatchingServices KERN_SUCCESS guard; a forced kernel failure must short-circuit before the iterator loop body runs (no registrySetCFProperty calls). Reachable via the AccelerometerKernelDriver injection seam.
mutations.replace: guard kr == kr else { return false }
mutations.search: guard kr == KERN_SUCCESS else { return false }

mutations.description: AccelerometerReader.SensorActivation.activate — invert the iterator sentinel; mock yields one synthetic service then 0, so the gate must let the loop body run on the service before breaking on 0. Inverting the comparison breaks before the body and the registry-write counter stays at 0.

AccelerometerReader.SensorActivation.deactivate — drop the IOServiceGetMatchingServices KERN_SUCCESS guard; forced kernel failure must short-circuit before the iterator loop body runs (no registrySetCFProperty calls).

AccelerometerReader.SensorActivation.deactivate — invert the iterator sentinel; mock yields one synthetic service then 0, so the gate must let the loop body run on the service before breaking on 0. Inverting the comparison breaks before the body and the registry-write counter stays at 0.
AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.isSPUDevicePresent — drop the IOHIDManagerOpen kIOReturnSuccess guard; forced manager-open failure must yield false and short-circuit before transport probing.
mutations.replace: guard driver.hidManagerOpen(manager) != driver.hidManagerOpen(manager) else { return false }
mutations.search: guard driver.hidManagerOpen(manager) == kIOReturnSuccess else { return false }

mutations.description: AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.isSensorActivelyReporting — drop the IOServiceGetMatchingServices KERN_SUCCESS guard; forced kernel failure must yield false and short-circuit before iterator polling.

AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.isSensorActivelyReporting — invert the iterator sentinel; mock yields one synthetic service then 0, so the gate must let the loop body probe dispatchAccel + DebugState before breaking on 0. Inverting the comparison breaks before the body and registryCreateCFProperty stays at 0.

AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.openStream — drop the IOHIDManagerOpen kIOReturnSuccess guard; forced manager-open failure must surface SensorError.ioKitError on the consumer stream and short-circuit before IOHIDDeviceOpen is reached.

AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.openStream — drop the IOHIDDeviceOpen kIOReturnSuccess guard; forced device-open failure must surface SensorError.ioKitError and short-circuit before hidDeviceMaxReportSize is reached.

AccelerometerReader.AccelHardware.openStream — drop the maxSize > 0 guard; mock returns 0 and the production path must surface SensorError.ioKitError with kIOReturnInternalError rather than allocate a zero-length buffer.
USBSource.start — drop notifyPort==nil idempotency guard; second start must NOT re-register IOKit matching notifications. _testInstallationCount tracks each successful install path.
mutations.replace:
if false { return }
        #if DEBUG
        self._testBus = bus
        #endif
        let port = IONotificationPortCreate(kIOMainPortDefault)
        IONotificationPortSetDispatchQueue(port, DispatchQueue.main)
        self.notifyPort = port
mutations.search:
guard notifyPort == nil else { return }
        #if DEBUG
        self._testBus = bus
        #endif
        let port = IONotificationPortCreate(kIOMainPortDefault)
        IONotificationPortSetDispatchQueue(port, DispatchQueue.main)
        self.notifyPort = port

mutations.description: USBSource.start — drop kernel-success guard; failed IOServiceAddMatchingNotification must NOT advance to publishTask install. _forceKernelFailureKr seam injects a non-success return so the cell observes whether the cleanup branch ran.
mutations.replace:
if false {
            log.warning("activity:USBSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:IOService — attachKr=\(attachKr) detachKr=\(detachKr)")
mutations.search:
guard attachKr == KERN_SUCCESS, detachKr == KERN_SUCCESS else {
            log.warning("activity:USBSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:IOService — attachKr=\(attachKr) detachKr=\(detachKr)")

mutations.description: AudioPeripheralSource.start — drop listenerInstalled idempotency guard; second start must NOT re-install the CoreAudio property listener.
AudioPeripheralSource.start — drop kernel-success guard; failed AudioObjectAddPropertyListenerBlock must NOT advance to listenerInstalled=true. _forceListenerStatus seam injects a non-noErr OSStatus.
mutations.search:
guard status == noErr else {
            log.warning("activity:AudioPeripheralSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:CoreAudio status=\(status)")

mutations.description: BluetoothSource.start — drop notifyPort==nil idempotency guard; second start must NOT re-register IOKit matching notifications.
BluetoothSource.start — drop kernel-success guard; failed IOServiceAddMatchingNotification must NOT advance to publishTask install.
mutations.replace:
if false {
            log.warning("activity:BluetoothSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:IOService — attachKr=\(attachKr) detachKr=\(detachKr)")
mutations.search:
guard attachKr == KERN_SUCCESS, detachKr == KERN_SUCCESS else {
            log.warning("activity:BluetoothSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:IOService — attachKr=\(attachKr) detachKr=\(detachKr)")

mutations.description: ThunderboltSource.start — drop notifyPort==nil idempotency guard; second start must NOT re-register IOKit matching notifications.
ThunderboltSource.start — drop kernel-success guard; failed IOServiceAddMatchingNotification must NOT advance to publishTask install.
mutations.replace:
if false {
            log.warning("activity:ThunderboltSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:IOService — attachKr=\(attachKr) detachKr=\(detachKr)")
mutations.search:
guard attachKr == KERN_SUCCESS, detachKr == KERN_SUCCESS else {
            log.warning("activity:ThunderboltSource wasInvalidatedBy entity:IOService — attachKr=\(attachKr) detachKr=\(detachKr)")

mutations.description: DisplayHotplugSource.start — drop !registered idempotency guard; second start must NOT re-register the CGDisplay reconfiguration callback.
SleepWakeSource.start — drop rootPort==0 idempotency guard; second start must NOT re-register IORegisterForSystemPower.

SleepWakeSource.start — drop the connect!=0 half of the kernel-success guard so a 0-connect failure proceeds; the residual let-port keeps the file syntactically valid. _forceRegistrationFailure seam injects a 0 connect; with the guard relaxed the cleanup branch is skipped and _testInstallationCount bumps.
MouseActivitySource.shouldDispatchClick — drop the result-success half of the result/context guard. Predicate now admits non-success IOReturns; cell asserts admit==false.
mutations.replace:
guard !contextIsNil else { return false }
        guard usagePage == 0x09
mutations.search:
guard result == kIOReturnSuccess, !contextIsNil else { return false }
        guard usagePage == 0x09

mutations.description: MouseActivitySource.shouldDispatchClick — drop the contextIsNil half of the result/context guard. Predicate now admits nil-context; cell asserts admit==false.
mutations.replace: guard result == kIOReturnSuccess else { return false }
mutations.search: guard result == kIOReturnSuccess, !contextIsNil else { return false }

mutations.description: MouseActivitySource.shouldDispatchClick — drop the usagePage==0x09 guard. Predicate now admits non-mouse usage pages.
mutations.replace: guard usage == 0x01, value != 0 else { return false }
mutations.search: guard usagePage == 0x09, usage == 0x01, value != 0 else { return false }

mutations.description: MouseActivitySource.shouldDispatchClick — drop the usage==0x01 button-1 guard. Predicate now admits non-button-1 usages.
mutations.replace:
guard usagePage == 0x09, value != 0 else { return false }
        return true
mutations.search:
guard usagePage == 0x09, usage == 0x01, value != 0 else { return false }
        return true

mutations.description: MouseActivitySource.shouldDispatchClick — drop the value!=0 guard so button releases (value=0) also dispatch.
MouseActivitySource.start — drop the double-start idempotency guard. Calling start() twice now installs the scrollWheel monitor twice; MockEventMonitor.installCount catches it.
KeyboardActivitySource.shouldDispatchKeyPress — drop the result-success half. Predicate now admits non-success IOReturns.
mutations.replace:
guard !contextIsNil else { return false }
        guard usagePage == 0x07
mutations.search:
guard result == kIOReturnSuccess, !contextIsNil else { return false }
        guard usagePage == 0x07

mutations.description: KeyboardActivitySource.shouldDispatchKeyPress — drop the contextIsNil half.
mutations.replace: guard result == kIOReturnSuccess else { return false }
mutations.search: guard result == kIOReturnSuccess, !contextIsNil else { return false }

mutations.description: KeyboardActivitySource.shouldDispatchKeyPress — drop the usagePage==0x07 keyboard-page guard.
mutations.replace: guard usage > 0, value != 0 else { return false }
mutations.search: guard usagePage == 0x07, usage > 0, value != 0 else { return false }

mutations.description: KeyboardActivitySource.shouldDispatchKeyPress — drop the usage>0 guard so zero-usage keys dispatch.
mutations.replace:
guard usagePage == 0x07, value != 0 else { return false }
        return true
mutations.search:
guard usagePage == 0x07, usage > 0, value != 0 else { return false }
        return true

mutations.description: KeyboardActivitySource.shouldDispatchKeyPress — drop the value!=0 guard so key releases dispatch.
TrackpadActivitySource.attemptFireContact — drop the dur<=contactMax gate. Test seam fires with a synthesized `now` 10s past contactStart; mutation lets it through and publishes .trackpadContact.
TrackpadActivitySource.evaluateCircle — drop the mag>2.0 floor. Tiny samples now accumulate and a sub-floor full revolution fires .trackpadCircling.
mutations.replace: if false { return }  // ignore tiny movements
mutations.search: guard mag > 2.0 else { return }  // ignore tiny movements

mutations.description: TrackpadActivitySource.evaluateCircle — drop the rotation+event-count threshold. Half-revolutions now fire .trackpadCircling.
TrackpadActivitySource.evaluateCircle — drop the circlingEnabled+gate guard. Disabled circling now fires when a full revolution accumulates.
Theme.AccordionCard.animationDuration(forRows:) — drop the 0.30s upper cap. Large row counts now exceed the easeInOut perceptual budget.
Theme.AccordionCard.animationDuration(forRows:) — drop the per-row scale (multiply by 0). Duration collapses to the floor for every row count.
Theme.AccordionCard.animationDuration(forRows:) — drop the rows>=1 floor. Negative row inputs produce sub-floor durations before the outer max(0.10) clamp.
Theme.SensorAccordionCard.animationDuration(forRows:) mirror — drop the 0.30s ceiling on the sensor card surface; mixed-card panels now animate at different speeds.
SettingsStore.sensitivityMin didSet — drop the recursive-clamp-and-return; out-of-range writes now persist instead of being snapped into [0,1].
mutations.search: if c != sensitivityMin { sensitivityMin = c; return }

mutations.description: SettingsStore.sensitivityMin didSet — drop the pair-fixup that drags max up when min exceeds it; the (min, max) ordering invariant breaks.
SettingsStore.sensitivityMax didSet — drop the pair-fixup that drags min down when max falls below it; (min, max) ordering invariant breaks.
SettingsStore.volumeMax didSet — drop the recursive-clamp-and-return; above-range writes now persist past the unit-range bound.
mutations.search: if c != volumeMax { volumeMax = c; return }

mutations.description: SettingsStore.flashOpacityMin didSet — drop the pair-fixup; min above max no longer drags max up, leaving an inverted opacity band.
SettingsStore.accelBandpassLowHz didSet — drop the pair-fixup that drags high up when low exceeds it; the bandpass filter is mis-applied.
SettingsStore.sanitizeNonFiniteAndPairings — drop the sensitivityMin isFinite check; a NaN persisted under the key now propagates into the live store.
SettingsStore.sanitizeNonFiniteAndPairings — drop the cold-load bandpass pair-fixup; a disk-corrupted (low > high) pair survives boot and the bandpass filter rejects every input.
SettingsStore.flashEnabled didSet — drop the legacy-mode sync that resets visualResponseMode to .off when flash is turned off; legacy consumers see stale .overlay even though flash is off.
SettingsStore.flashEnabled didSet — drop the legacy-mode sync that promotes visualResponseMode to .overlay when flash is turned on; legacy consumers see stale .off even though flash is on.
RangeSlider — collapse the default labelWidth from 40 to 0; default-call sites lose all label real estate and the slider's intrinsic width drops by 80pt.
SensitivityRuler — zero out the GeometryReader's vertical frame; the ruler must retain its 16pt vertical footprint so the menu-bar layout stays stable.

SensitivityRuler — collapse the LEFT-gutter Spacer width from 50 to 0; the menu-bar tick region must keep its left padding so labels never crowd the panel edge. Multi-line search disambiguates the left gutter (followed by the GeometryReader) from the right gutter (followed by the HStack close).

RangeSlider.clamp(position:half:usable:) — drop the lower-bound max() so an overshoot-left x-coordinate runs negative; clamp must hold the position at `half` regardless of how far left the user drags.

RangeSlider.clamp(position:half:usable:) — drop the upper-bound min() so an overshoot-right x-coordinate runs past the track; clamp must hold the position at `half + usable` regardless of how far right the user drags.
RangeSlider.applyDrag — disable the low-overshoots-high pair-swap branch so a drag of the low thumb past the high thumb leaves the (low, high) pair in an invalid low > high state.
mutations.replace:
if false {
                return DragResult(low: high, high: value, active: .high)
mutations.search:
if value > high {
                return DragResult(low: high, high: value, active: .high)

mutations.description: RangeSlider.applyDrag — disable the high-overshoots-low pair-swap branch so a drag of the high thumb past the low thumb leaves the (low, high) pair in an invalid high < low state.
mutations.replace:
if false {
                return DragResult(low: value, high: low, active: .low)
mutations.search:
if value < low {
                return DragResult(low: value, high: low, active: .low)

mutations.description: RangeSlider.applyDrag — flip the overlap-disambiguation translation-sign comparator so a positive-direction drag on overlapped thumbs grabs the LOW thumb instead of HIGH (and vice versa); the wrong thumb leads under thumb overlap.

SensitivityRuler.ticks — mutate the medium-tier position from 0.50 to 0.40; the five tier marks must lie at the canonical [0.0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0] unit-interval positions so the ruler aligns with the slider track endpoints and SettingsStore tier thresholds.
mutations.replace: Tick(position: 0.40, label: NSLocalizedString("tier_medium", comment: "Ruler label: medium impact (abbreviated)")),
mutations.search: Tick(position: 0.50, label: NSLocalizedString("tier_medium", comment: "Ruler label: medium impact (abbreviated)")),

mutations.description: SensitivityRuler.position(for:in:) — collapse the per-tick placement formula to 0; every tick must sit at `tick.position * width` so the five tier marks spread across the available width instead of piling up at the origin.

Updater.currentVersion(bundle:) — mutate the App-Store-stub fallback literal from "1.0.0" to "0.0.0"; when Bundle.infoDictionary is nil the helper must return the canonical "1.0.0" so the menu-bar footer never displays a degraded version. Multi-line search anchors on the App-Store-branch doc comment to disambiguate from the identical literal in the DIRECT_BUILD branch.

Updater.isNewer SemVer 2.0 §11.3 — flip the (nil, _?) case to return false so a release version no longer ranks above its own pre-release with the same core. Cell V asserts release "1.2.3" > pre-release "1.2.3-rc1"; this mutation regresses to the pre-7c "by-accident" answer where the pre-release branch was unreachable.

Updater.isNewer SemVer 2.0 §11.4.2 — invert the alphanumeric pre-release identifier comparison so ASCII order is reversed. Cell W asserts "rc2" > "rc1" and "rc1" > "alpha1" by ASCII lex; this mutation flips both comparisons in the (nil, nil) branch (string identifiers) so the comparator returns the opposite ordering.
mutations.replace:
if a < b { return .orderedDescending }
                if a > b { return .orderedAscending }
mutations.search:
if a < b { return .orderedAscending }
                if a > b { return .orderedDescending }

mutations.description: Updater.isNewer SemVer 2.0 §11.4.1 — break the numeric pre-release identifier comparison by routing it through the string operands instead of integer values. Cell X asserts "rc.10" > "rc.2" by INTEGER comparison (string lex would give the wrong answer because "10" < "2" lexicographically). The mutation swaps `x < y` / `x > y` for `a < b` / `a > b`, forcing string lex on numeric identifiers and inverting the test's expected ordering.
mutations.replace:
if a < b { return .orderedAscending }
                if a > b { return .orderedDescending }
mutations.search:
if x < y { return .orderedAscending }
                if x > y { return .orderedDescending }

mutations.description: FlowLayout.balancedRowCounts — flip the smaller-row-on-top ordering so 5 → [3, 2] instead of [2, 3].
FlowLayout.balancedRowCounts — replace ceiling with floor division; 5/4 collapses to 1 row, 13/4 to 3.
FlowLayout.sizeThatFits — drop the inter-row spacing accumulator; multi-row reports collapse by (rows-1)*spacing.
mutations.search: let totalH = rowH * CGFloat(counts.count) + spacing * CGFloat(max(0, counts.count - 1))

mutations.description: FlowLayout.uniformRowHeight — flip max to min so the global row height shrinks to the smallest child rather than the largest.
mutations.replace: for s in subviews { h = min(h == 0 ? .infinity : h, s.sizeThatFits(.unspecified).height) }
mutations.search: for s in subviews { h = max(h, s.sizeThatFits(.unspecified).height) }

mutations.description: GyroDetector.process — drop sample-count warmup gate; samples within warmup window must NOT publish .gyroSpike.
mutations.search: guard s.sampleCount >= config.warmupSamples else { return nil }

mutations.description: GyroDetector.process — drop spike-threshold magnitude floor; sub-threshold magnitudes must NOT publish .gyroSpike.
mutations.search: guard magnitude > config.spikeThreshold else { return nil }

mutations.description: GyroDetector.process — drop minConfirmations gate; under-confirmation single sample must NOT publish .gyroSpike.
GyroscopeSource.handleReport — drop debounce window; tight burst of above-threshold reports must coalesce to one fire.
mutations.search: if let last = s.lastFiredAt, timestamp.timeIntervalSince(last) < ReactionsConfig.gyroDebounce {

mutations.description: GyroscopeSource.handleReport — corrupt the X-axis byte offset (canonical 6 → 0); decoded magnitude collapses to zero (the test payload has zero-bytes at offsets 0..5) and the cell-expected single fire never appears.
